metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC5 that, using the same stub harness, drives the success path and asserts exactly one `builder prune -f --keep-storage 5GB` and one `image prune -f` are logged — proving `prune_docker_caches` is wired on both paths.
findings.finding: The success-path replacement (replacing the bare `docker image prune -f` on the success path with `prune_docker_caches`) has no acceptance criterion — all four ACs only verify the failure path (AC1/AC2 drive `record_backoff_failure`) or static properties (AC3/AC4). An executor could skip the success-path change and every AC would still pass green.
findings.location: What section ('Replace the success path's bare `docker image prune -f` with the same helper') / Acceptance Criteria (no corresponding AC)
findings.suggested_fix: AC5 (success path prunes): with the docker stub active, trigger the success-path code (e.g. call the function or code block that previously held the bare `docker image prune -f`); assert the stub log contains exactly one `builder prune -f --keep-storage 5GB` and one `image prune -f` invocation and zero bare `image prune` invocations without a preceding `builder prune`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC1) that positively verifies the success path invokes `prune_docker_caches()`, e.g. by stubbing docker and driving the success-path code, asserting `builder prune -f --keep-storage` appears in the stub log.
findings.finding: The described deliverable 'Replace the success path's bare `docker image prune -f` with the same helper' has no AC that directly verifies it. AC1 only drives `record_backoff_failure` (failure path). AC3 is a negative grep (no unguarded `docker builder prune`) which would catch a stray bare call but does not positively assert the success path invokes `prune_docker_caches()`. An implementer could leave the success path unchanged and all four ACs would still pass.
findings.location: What section ('Replace the success path's bare docker image prune -f with the same helper') vs AC1–AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC5: 'With the docker stub active, driving the success path (simulating a healthy deploy completion) records exactly one `builder prune -f --keep-storage 5GB` and one `image prune -f` invocation, confirming the helper replaced the bare success-path prune.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC3/AC4) that verifies the success-path prune now calls `prune_docker_caches` (e.g., grep that the old bare `docker image prune -f` no longer appears outside the helper, or a stub test that drives the success exit path and records the builder prune invocation).
findings.finding: The success-path change — replacing the bare `docker image prune -f` with `prune_docker_caches()` — is a non-deferred goal stated in the 'What' section but no AC explicitly verifies it was applied; the executor could skip it and all four ACs would still pass.
findings.location: What section, paragraph 3 ('Replace the success path's bare docker image prune -f…'); Acceptance Criteria (AC1–AC4)
findings.suggested_fix: Add: AC5 (success path prunes): with the docker stub active, driving a successful deploy exit records exactly one `builder prune -f --keep-storage 5GB` and one `image prune -f` on the success path — or extend AC3 to also assert that `docker image prune -f` does not appear outside the `prune_docker_caches` function body.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC verifying the success path now invokes `prune_docker_caches` (e.g., with the stub, drive the success path and assert exactly one `builder prune -f --keep-storage 5GB` and one `image prune -f` are logged, and the bare `docker image prune -f` call no longer appears without the builder prune).
findings.finding: The plan states the success-path's bare `docker image prune -f` will be replaced by `prune_docker_caches()` (adding `builder prune --keep-storage` on success), but no acceptance criterion verifies that this replacement was actually made — all ACs focus exclusively on `record_backoff_failure`. An executor could omit the success-path change and all four ACs would still pass.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — success path; 'What' section: 'Replace the success path's bare docker image prune -f with the same helper'
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC5: 'With the stub active, drive the success path (mock health-check passes); assert the stub log contains exactly one `builder prune -f --keep-storage 5GB` and one `image prune -f` invocation, and that no bare `docker image prune -f` without a preceding `builder prune` in the same helper call exists in the script (grep `autodeploy.sh` for standalone `docker image prune` outside the helper definition).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the prose grep description in AC3 with a concrete anchored command, e.g.: `grep -P 'docker builder prune' infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh | grep -qv -- '--keep-storage' && echo FAIL || echo PASS` — or better, `! grep -E 'docker[[:space:]]+builder[[:space:]]+prune' infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh | grep -vE '\-\-keep-storage'` — to eliminate substring false-positives and comment-line leakage.
findings.finding: AC3's proving command is described only in prose ('asserted by grep that no `docker builder prune` invocation exists without `--keep-storage`') with no concrete anchored grep command, creating a substring false-positive risk (defect type 1 of the verify-command taxonomy): a bare `grep 'builder prune'` would match comment lines or lines that include `--keep-storage` anywhere in a multi-line construct.
findings.suggested_fix: AC3 proving command: `! grep -E '^[^#]*docker[[:space:]]+builder[[:space:]]+prune' infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh | grep -qvE -- '--keep-storage'` — this anchors to non-comment lines and asserts every `docker builder prune` invocation includes `--keep-storage`.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `warn`-level log line (to stderr, not suppressed) when either prune command exits non-zero inside `prune_docker_caches()`, so operators can detect silent mitigation failures without masking the deploy exit code (e.g., `docker builder prune ... 2>&1 | { grep -q . && echo '[autodeploy] WARN: builder prune failed'; } || true`).
findings.finding: Prune failures are fully silenced (`>/dev/null 2>&1 || true`) with no log line, metric, or alert, so if `docker builder prune` repeatedly fails (daemon restart, buildkit disabled, permissions), the disk-pressure mitigation silently does nothing and operators have no signal until the next ENOSPC incident.
findings.location: What section — both prune invocations; AC2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify `--keep-storage` against the actual `docker builder prune --help` output on the target box (or pin the minimum Docker version that introduces this flag), and add a startup check or CI step that confirms the flag is accepted — not just present in source.
findings.finding: `docker builder prune --keep-storage` flag name is Partially-verified: the plan asserts `--keep-storage` as the flag that enforces the hard storage cap (the plan's entire bounded-growth guarantee rests on it, and AC3 greps for it), but this flag name cannot be confirmed against live `docker builder prune --help` output in this environment, and the installed Docker version on the box is not pinned anywhere in the repo. If the flag is absent or renamed in the installed version, the cap silently does not apply (swallowed by `|| true`) and the disk-pressure hardening goal fails without any observable signal.
findings.location: What section, step 1: `docker builder prune -f --keep-storage "${BUILD_CACHE_KEEP:-5GB}"`; also AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-time probe in the plan's test notes (or as a commented assertion in the tunables block): `docker builder prune --help 2>&1 | grep -q -- '--keep-storage' || echo 'WARNING: --keep-storage flag not supported; BUILD_CACHE_KEEP cap will not apply'`. Also document the minimum Docker version required (≥23.0 where `--keep-storage` is confirmed stable) in the tunables comment.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale for the after-failure timing choice (vs. before-attempt), or consider also calling `prune_docker_caches` at the start of the bot-build block (before `docker compose build`) to cap pre-existing legacy cache before it contributes to a new disk spike.
findings.finding: The plan provides no positive rationale for pruning AFTER failure (inside `record_backoff_failure`) versus pruning BEFORE each build attempt (at the top of the bot-build block, before `docker compose build`). Pruning before-attempt has a concrete advantage: it clears legacy-accumulated cache from prior incident conditions before the next failing rebuild consumes disk — directly relevant to the post-incident recovery scenario (a box carrying multi-GB cache from 41h of failures would not benefit from the after-failure prune until the FIRST new failure fires, during which the full build re-adds up to the legacy amount).
findings.location: ## What — 'Call `prune_docker_caches` from `record_backoff_failure()`' and 'Replace the success path's bare `docker image prune -f` with the same helper'
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, add: 'Pruning after failure (rather than before each build attempt) is chosen for testability — AC1 can exercise the single `record_backoff_failure` seam — and because `--keep-storage 5GB` ensures the steady-state cap is identical. To also protect the transient post-accumulation scenario, `prune_docker_caches` MAY additionally be called just before `docker compose build` (before the cache-accumulating operation); this is left as a follow-up unless disk-pressure alerting (ticket ac14) is in place first.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
